project: Papaya Ring Spot Virus Knowledge Base

chunks.title: PRSV overview
chunks.text: Papaya Ring Spot Virus is a major viral disease affecting papaya plants. It can reduce yield, weaken plant vigor, and cause visible leaf and fruit symptoms that intensify over time.

chunks.title: Leaf symptom pattern
chunks.text: Common PRSV leaf symptoms include mosaic patterns, chlorosis, mottling, distortion, and irregular texture changes. Diseased leaves may show visually abnormal regions compared to healthy green tissue.

chunks.title: Chlorosis interpretation
chunks.text: Reduced green intensity and chlorotic appearance can indicate stress or viral symptom development. In papaya leaves, abnormal yellowish regions may support suspicion of PRSV-related damage.

chunks.title: Texture and irregularity
chunks.text: Higher texture irregularity, edge-rich damaged regions, and visually inconsistent patches can correspond to symptomatic leaf areas. These patterns are useful for image-based disease interpretation.

chunks.title: Severity progression
chunks.text: As disease severity increases, symptomatic area coverage, discoloration, and visual irregularity often become more prominent. Moderate and severe stages may require rapid field intervention.

chunks.title: Vector spread note
chunks.text: PRSV can spread through aphid vectors. If one plant shows suspicious symptoms, nearby plants should also be checked so that spread risk can be reduced early.

chunks.title: Field advisory
chunks.text: When papaya leaves show suspicious disease symptoms, monitor nearby plants, remove heavily affected material carefully when advised locally, and seek agronomy verification before large-scale action.

chunks.title: Research interpretation note
chunks.text: A research-oriented PRSV diagnostic system should connect image observations, feature values, severity estimation, and retrieved domain evidence to produce grounded and explainable outputs.

chunks.title: Mosaic and discoloration note
chunks.text: Mosaic-like appearance and leaf discoloration are important visual indicators in PRSV analysis. Image-processing methods that measure color change and irregular structural response can help support diagnosis.

chunks.title: Farmer-friendly response guidance
chunks.text: If symptoms continue spreading across several leaves, ask an agricultural expert for confirmation and keep photo records. Early observation can help reduce crop loss.
